figure.title: Address Caching System - Rx Caches
figure.purpose: Illustrates the address caching mechanism for incoming traffic, indexed by Source Accelerator ID (SRCACCID).

technical_summary: Shows a source (SRC) Accelerator, a Switch, and a destination (DST) Accelerator.
The SRC Accelerator has an Rx Cache indexed by SRCACCID in its UALink TL.
The Switch has Tx Caches, also indexed by SRCACCID, at its egress ports.
The diagram traces the flow of cached address information from the DST Accelerator (acting as originator for this flow) through the switch to the SRC Accelerator.
Green arrows indicate the path of the compressed/cached addresses from the transmitter to the receiver.

components.name: Accelerator Rx Cache
components.description: Cache in the receiving node used to decompress incoming addresses.

components.name: Switch Tx Cache
components.description: Cache in the switch egress port to compress outgoing addresses.

critical_facts: While Tx caches (for outgoing requests) are indexed by DSTACCID, Rx caches (and Switch Tx caches for forwarding) are indexed by SRCACCID to manage reverse or incoming traffic compression.
